channel.title: OOIR Trends: Clinical Medicine (Orthopedics)
channel.description: Aktuelle Paper-Trends im Bereich Clinical Medicine (Orthopedics) von OOIR (mit Titel und Metadaten von Crossref)

item.title: The Prognosis of Disabling Musculoskeletal Pain and Predictors of Recovery in Children
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Orthopedics Score: 40 Autoren: Veronica Souza Santos, Mariana Nascimento Leite, Steven J. Kamper, Caio Sain Vallio, Aidan G. Cashin, Fabianna Resende Jesus-Moraleida, Tuyra Francisca Castro E. Silva, Luciola da Cunha Menezes Costa, Leonardo Oliveira Pena Costa, Luiz Hespanhol, Chris G. Maher, Tiê Parma Yamato Journal: Journal of Orthopaedic & Sports Physical Therapy Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 Abstract: OBJECTIVE: To (1) describe the course of disabling musculoskeletal pain in children and adolescents in an 18-month follow-up, and (2) develop a prediction model to identify those most likely to recover. DESIGN: Prospective cohort study. METHODS: We recruited children and adolescents with disabling musculoskeletal pain from schools in Brazil. We collected information about sociodemographic characteristics and biopsychosocial variables. Recovery and recurrence were reported as frequencies and percentages over time. Prediction models were developed through logistic mixed models with candidate variables selected using a theoretical model. We measured the performance of the model and the internal validity. RESULTS: We included 694 children and adolescents with a mean age of 12.2 years (standard deviation, 2.7). Of those, 85.9% (95% confidence interval [CI]: 82.8, 88.6) recovered from disabling pain. From those who recovered, 31.7% (95% CI: 27.6, 36) suffered a recurrence. The prediction model identified cases of recovery from disabling musculoskeletal pain, with an area under the curve of 84.1% (95% CI: 79.8, 87.8), an accuracy of 0.55 (95% CI: 0.51, 0.58), and a sensitivity of 0.72 (95% CI: 0.67, 0.78). The model was assertive in 6 out of every 10 positive cases based on the positive predictive value. CONCLUSION: Up to 9 in every 10 children recovered from pain, and pain recurrence was common. The prediction model tested was adequate to identify children and adolescents who were more likely to recover from disabling musculoskeletal pain. J Orthop Sports Phys Ther 2026;56(6):359-369. Epub 27 March 2026. doi:10.2519/jospt.2026.13817 DOI: 10.2519/jospt.2026.13817 ISSN: 0190-6011 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-01

item.title: Emergency department burden of e-scooter injuries in Victoria, Australia: A statewide analysis of injury patterns and prevention implications, 2022–2025
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Orthopedics Score: 36 Autoren: Lisa N. Sharwood, Jane Hayman, Ehsan Rezaei-Darzi, Caitlin Laska, David Eager, Alexandra Martiniuk Journal: Injury Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.injury.2026.113446 ISSN: 0020-1383 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-01

item.title: Many Paths to Recovery: The Case for Treatment Pluralism
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Orthopedics Score: 33 Autoren: Jared K. Powell, Amy McDevitt, Chad Cook Journal: Journal of Orthopaedic & Sports Physical Therapy Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 Abstract: SYNOPSIS: Decades of research have not identified a consistently superior treatment for musculoskeletal (MSK) pain conditions. Across trials, interventions such as exercise therapy and manual techniques demonstrate modest, broadly equivalent average treatment effects, likely mediated by overlapping, nonspecific, and contextual mechanisms. Treatment pluralism provides a practical way to interpret this evidence. It acknowledges that while treatments may appear comparable at the group level, substantial heterogeneity in treatment effects means that individual patients can respond quite differently. Frameworks such as the person-centered hypothesis aim to integrate pain phenotype, context, and pattern recognition to refine treatment selection. For now, predictive precision remains aspirational. Pluralism encourages a bounded range of safe, plausible, evidence-informed treatment options; it allows clinicians to practice with different orientations provided these are communicated transparently and aligned with patient values, goals, and preferences. Rather than enforcing uniformity, embracing pluralism offers an honest and patient-centered way to navigate uncertainty in MSK care. J Orthop Sports Phys Ther 2026;56(6):331-333. Epub 25 March 2026. doi:10.2519/jospt.2026.13992 DOI: 10.2519/jospt.2026.13992 ISSN: 0190-6011 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-01

item.title: Artificial intelligence–driven data expansion for the validation of spinopelvic parameter correlations in asymptomatic subjects
item.description: Feld: Clinical Medicine Kategorie: Orthopedics Score: 18 Autoren: Domenico Compagnone, Davide Lamartina, Marco Giacalone, Riccardo Cecchinato, Pedro Berjano, Claudio Lamartina Journal: European Spine Journal Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-02 DOI: 10.1007/s00586-026-10051-x ISSN: 0940-6719 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-01

item.title: Re-triage of severely injured patients in no-fault vs. at-fault states

Thomas, Susheel Reddy, Mira Ghneim, Jessica L. Ryan, Etienne L. Pracht, Marie L. Crandall, Alexander Lundberg, Anne M. Stey Journal: Injury Veröffentlicht: 2026-06-01 DOI: 10.1016/j.injury.2026.113466 ISSN: 0020-1383 Tag der Erhebung (OOIR): 2026-07-01
